info.title: Revolut Wallet Partner API
info.description:
Endpoints hosted by Revolut that partners call to put passes into Revolut Wallet and keep
them current.

- **Create a pass**: `POST /passes` registers a pass and returns its Revolut `id`.
- **Push a pass update**: `PUT /passes/{id}` replaces the pass with its latest state and fans
  it out to every holder.

You give each pass a **`partner_reference`**, which is your own key to match it in your system, and the
value Revolut uses when it calls the endpoints you host (see the companion Wallet Partner Callbacks API,
`/docs/api/revolut-wallet-callbacks`). Revolut assigns its own pass `id`, returned when you create a
pass; use that `id` to address the pass on `PUT /passes/{id}`.

:::info
Start with the [Revolut Wallet guide](https://developer.revolut.com/docs/guides/revolut-wallet/introduction) and [Get started](https://developer.revolut.com/docs/guides/revolut-wallet/get-started) to onboard your business.
:::

tags.name: Passes
tags.description: Create passes and push updates into Revolut Wallet.

post.description:
Create a pass in Revolut Wallet from its semantic content. Returns the Revolut-assigned pass
`id`.

Set `partner_reference` to your own key for the pass. Revolut uses it when it polls you for
updates. Use the returned `id` to push updates (`PUT /passes/{id}`). Re-using a
`partner_reference` that already exists returns `409`.

:::info
See [Create a pass](https://developer.revolut.com/docs/guides/revolut-wallet/operations/create-pass) for a step-by-step walkthrough.
:::

put.description:
Replace the stored pass with the latest version from your system. Send the **complete**
pass. This is a full replacement, not a partial update. Bump `version` on every change.

Address the pass by **either** the Revolut pass `id` (from the create response) **or** your
own `partner_reference` prefixed with `ref:`. Addressing by `partner_reference` is the only
option for passes created by auto-provisioning, where Revolut's `id` was never returned to
you. Bump `version` on every change to avoid overwriting a newer version.

:::info
See [Update a pass](https://developer.revolut.com/docs/guides/revolut-wallet/operations/update-pass) for push vs poll and implementation details.
:::

parameters.name: id
parameters.description:
The pass to update. Accepts **either**:
- the Revolut pass id (UUID), e.g. `3f9a1c2e-8b7d-4e6a-9f10-2c3b4d5e6f70`; or
- your `partner_reference`, prefixed with `ref:`, e.g. `ref:skyjet-2241-R7K92M`.

Always use the `ref:` prefix when addressing by your own reference. A bare value is
interpreted as the Revolut id.

oauth2.description:
OAuth 2.0 **client credentials**. Request a short-lived access token from the token endpoint
(authenticating with your onboarding credential: `client_secret`, `private_key_jwt`, or your
mTLS certificate) and send it as `Authorization: Bearer <token>`. The token carries the
`passes.write` scope. May be used on its own or together with mTLS.

jws.name: Jws-Signature
jws.description:
**Message signing (JWS).** Sign the request body together with a timestamp (to prevent
replay) as a detached JWS (`RS256`/`ES256`) using the private key whose certificate you
registered during onboarding, and send the compact JWS in the `Jws-Signature` header.
Revolut verifies it against your registered certificate. May be used on its own or together
with mTLS.

mtls.description:
**Mutual TLS.** Present a client certificate signed by the Revolut Partner CA from
onboarding; your Partner ID is bound to it. Can be used on its own, or layered on top of
OAuth 2.0 or JWS for defence in depth.

ApiVersion.name: Revolut-Api-Version
ApiVersion.description:
Dated API version. Currently `2026-07-01`. Sent on every request.
:::note
If you receive a version you do not implement, respond with `400`.
:::

IdempotencyKey.name: Idempotency-Key
IdempotencyKey.description:
UUID scoped to your Partner ID. Replaying a request with the same key and body returns the
original outcome; the same key with a different body returns `409`. Retained ≥ 7 days.

RequestId.name: Request-Id
RequestId.description: Your correlation id for this request. Revolut echoes it back in the response.
Unique id for this request; quote it in support requests.

CreatePassResult.description:
Response body for `POST /passes`. Contains the Revolut-assigned pass `id` and your
`partner_reference` echoed back.

id.description: Revolut-assigned pass id (UUID). Use it to address the pass on `PUT /passes/{id}`.

Error.description:
Standard error body for 4xx/5xx responses. `violations` is present on `422` validation
errors.

Violation.description: A single validation failure within an `Error.violations` array.

pointer.description: JSON Pointer to the field when location is `body`.

default.description: Fallback text when no localisation matches.

localisation_key.description: Key resolved against Revolut's localisation catalogue at render time.

Action.description: A button rendered on the pass (e.g. "Open website", "Get directions").

id.description: Unique identifier for the action within this pass.

type.description: Documented values: `open_external_url`. External URL domain needs to be registered at onboarding.

label.description: Display label for the button, with optional localisation.

importance.description: Relative prominence of this action (1 = primary, 5 = least prominent).

Presentation.description: How a trigger is presented to the customer.

title.description: Title text for the notification or tile, with optional localisation.

description.description: Body text for the notification or tile, with optional localisation.

Trigger.description: A context-aware notification that nudges the customer (e.g. proximity alert, time before event).

id.description: Unique identifier for the trigger within this pass.

importance.description: Relative prominence of this trigger (1 = high, 5 = low).

time_offset.description: ISO 8601 duration before the target (e.g. `PT1H` for 1 hour before departure).

radius_meters.description: Radius in meters around the `location_targets` coordinates for the proximity alert.

field_target.description: Name of the semantic field to watch for changes (e.g. `boarding.gate`).

presentation.description: How the trigger is presented to the customer (notification, tile).

PassBase.description: Fields common to every pass type.

partner_reference.description: Your own reference for the pass; use it to match the pass in your system.

type.description:
Pass type discriminator. Selects the `semantics` structure. Documented values:
`boarding_pass`, `event_pass`, `loyalty_pass`. New pass types are introduced in new API versions.

version.description: Your monotonically increasing version; bump on every change.

relevant_at.description: When the pass becomes time-relevant (departure, event start); drives polling cadence.

triggers.description: Context-aware notifications that nudge the customer.

Passenger.description: Passenger details for a boarding pass.

full_name.description: Full name of the passenger as it appears on the boarding pass.

fare_class.description: Human-readable fare class (e.g. Economy, Business, First).

fare_class_code.description: IATA fare class code (single letter, e.g. Y for Economy, J for Business).

frequent_flyer_program.description: Name of the frequent flyer program the passenger belongs to.

frequent_flyer_number.description: Membership number within the frequent flyer program.

name.description: Full name of the airport (e.g. London Luton Airport).

city.description: City where the airport is located.

Flight.description: Flight details for a boarding pass.

flight_number.description: Flight number without the carrier code (e.g. 2241, not SJ2241).

carrier_code.description: IATA or ICAO carrier code (2 alphanumeric characters).

carrier_name.description: Display name of the operating carrier.

operating_carrier_code.description: Carrier code of the actual operating carrier (for codeshare flights, this may differ from `carrier_code`).

aircraft_type.description: IATA aircraft type code (e.g. A320, B738).

Boarding.description: Boarding details for a flight — gate, seat, and boarding time.

gate.description: Boarding gate at the departure airport.

boarding_group.description: Boarding group or zone (airline-specific, e.g. A, B, 1, 2).

sequence_number.description: Boarding sequence number assigned to the passenger.

seat_class.description: Seat class label (e.g. Economy, Premium Economy, Business).

Booking.description: Booking details for a boarding pass.

pnr.description: Passenger Name Record — the booking reference from the airline's reservation system.

booking_reference.description: Alternative booking reference (e.g. agency or GDS reference), if different from the PNR.

Validity.description: Validity window for a pass — when it becomes active and when it expires.

valid_from.description: Start of the pass validity period, UTC, ISO 8601.

valid_until.description: End of the pass validity period, UTC, ISO 8601.

BoardingPassSemantics.description: Semantic tags for a boarding pass.

validity.description: Optional validity window for the pass.

BoardingPassCreate.description: A boarding pass being created, comprising the pass content plus its render template.

template_id.description:
Identifier of a pre-registered branding/layout template to render this pass with.
If omitted, Revolut uses the default template for your partner account, which may
not match your brand. Including it ensures your pass is rendered with the correct
visual design. Set at creation and immutable; not accepted when updating a pass.

Event.description: Event details for an event pass.

edition.description: Edition or iteration of a recurring event (e.g. 2026, Season 5).

held_on.description: Calendar day of the event (use `starts_at` for the precise start time).

starts_at.description: Start time of the event, UTC, ISO 8601.

ends_at.description: End time of the event, UTC, ISO 8601.

doors_open_at.description: Time the venue doors open, UTC, ISO 8601.

city.description: City where the venue is located.

section.description: Section or block within the venue (e.g. Block 112, Section C).

Ticket.description: Ticket details for an event pass.

booking_reference.description: Booking or order reference for the ticket purchase.

Participant.description: Participant details for an event pass (e.g. conference attendee).

company.description: Company or organisation the participant represents.

role.description: Role or title at the event (e.g. Speaker, Attendee, Volunteer).

name.description: Name of the organising company or individual.

EventPassSemantics.description: Semantic tags for an event pass.

validity.description: Optional validity window for the pass.

EventPassCreate.description: An event pass being created, comprising the pass content plus its render template.

template_id.description:
Identifier of a pre-registered branding/layout template to render this pass with.
If omitted, Revolut uses the default template for your partner account, which may
not match your brand. Including it ensures your pass is rendered with the correct
visual design. Set at creation and immutable; not accepted when updating a pass.

Member.description: Member details for a loyalty pass.

full_name.description: Full name of the loyalty program member.

member_id.description: Membership number or ID within the loyalty program.

member_since_on.description: Date the member joined the programme.

TierProgress.description: Progress towards the next loyalty tier.

current.description: Current progress value as a string (e.g. points earned this period).

target.description: Target value required to reach the next tier, as a string.

unit.description: Unit of measurement for the progress values (e.g. points, stays, flights).

Money.description: ISO 4217 monetary amount, represented as strings to preserve decimal precision.

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code (e.g. GBP, EUR, USD).

PointsBalance.description: Points balance for a loyalty pass.

value.description: Current points balance as a string (to preserve integer precision).

unit.description: Unit label for the balance (e.g. points, miles).

monetary_value.description: Optional monetary equivalent of the points balance.

NextReward.description: Next reward the member can unlock with their points.

description.description: Human-readable description of the reward (e.g. "Free coffee voucher").

points_required.description: Points required to unlock the reward, as a string.

Loyalty.description: Loyalty program details for a pass.

program_name.description: Display name of the loyalty program.

tier.description: Current tier level of the member (e.g. Gold, Silver, Bronze).

next_reward.description: Next reward the member can unlock.

LoyaltyPassSemantics.description: Semantic tags for a loyalty pass.

loyalty.description: Loyalty program details (tier, balance, next reward).

validity.description: Optional validity window for the pass.

LoyaltyPassCreate.description: A loyalty pass being created, comprising the pass content plus its render template.

template_id.description:
Identifier of a pre-registered branding/layout template to render this pass with.
If omitted, Revolut uses the default template for your partner account, which may
not match your brand. Including it ensures your pass is rendered with the correct
visual design. Set at creation and immutable; not accepted when updating a pass.

PassCreate.description:
A pass as created/provisioned, i.e. a concrete pass type plus the `template_id` that renders it.
Used when a pass is created (`POST /passes`) or provisioned from a transaction outcome. New pass types
may be added over time and are available in all supported API versions.

PassUpdate.description:
The updatable state of a pass, i.e. one of the concrete types selected by `type`. Used both when you
push an update and when Revolut polls you for the latest state. Excludes `template_id`, which is
fixed at creation. New pass types may be added over time and are available in all supported API versions.

Unauthorized.description:
Missing or invalid credentials: no or failed OAuth token, an invalid JWS signature, or a
missing/invalid client certificate.

value.message: The access token is expired or invalid.

Forbidden.description:
Authenticated, but the credential lacks the required scope/permission (e.g. an OAuth token
without `passes.write`). For OAuth, accompanied by `WWW-Authenticate: Bearer
error="insufficient_scope"`.

value.message: The access token does not have the passes.write scope.

NotFound.description:
No pass with this `id` exists under your Partner ID. A pass owned by another partner is
also reported as `404`. Existence of passes you don't own is never disclosed.

value.message: No pass with this id exists under your Partner ID.

Conflict.description:
The `id` already exists (on create), the pass `version` is stale, or an `Idempotency-Key`
was reused with a different body.

value.message: The version does not match the stored version +1.

ContentTooLarge.description: The pass payload exceeds the size limit.

value.message: The pass payload exceeds the size limit.

UnprocessableEntity.description: Well-formed, but the pass cannot be created/updated in its current state.

value.message: The pass cannot be created in its current state.
Rate limit exceeded. Retry after the specified delay.
